title: 10 best herbal capsules for post nasal drip

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain the herb Echinacea, which has been traditionally used to support immune system function and reduce inflammation. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate congestion and sinus pressure associated with post nasal drip, while also helping to boost the body's natural defenses against infection. Additionally, Kansas coneflower's antimicrobial properties may help combat bacterial or viral infections that can contribute to post nasal drip.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas Coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like cichoric acid, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating post-nasal drip symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Kansas Coneflower capsules, such as polyphenols and flavonoids, help protect the mucous membranes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the severity of post-nasal drip.

Immune system support: Kansas Coneflower has been traditionally used to support immune function, which can help combat underlying infections or allergies that may be contributing to post-nasal drip, thereby alleviating symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain triterpene saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe and dry up post nasal drip.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried echinacea angustifolia roots and stems of 250-500 grams weight.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by rinsing with filtered water and air-drying for 24 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered echinacea angustifolia with 500mg of gelatin capsules and fill each capsule with a precise 400mg dosage.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months' duration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat and nasal passages. The licorice root extract found in these capsules has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and congestion, making it an effective remedy for relieving the symptoms of postnasal drip. By reducing swelling and irritation, herbal licorice capsules can help to alleviate discomfort and promote a sense of clearness and ease in the nasal passages.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and discomfort associated with postnasal drip.

Mucolytic properties: Herbal licorice capsules contain mucolytic agents that break down thick mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing the sensation of a lump or cough associated with postnasal drip.

Expectorant properties: Licorice root has natural expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from congestion and coughing that often accompanies postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nose and throat.
Bark: Contains compounds like liquorice acid and isoliquiritigenin, which have anti-inflammatory properties to reduce nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots or purchase dried root powder from a reputable source.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 3 hours to preserve medicinal properties.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix one part ground licorice root with two parts vegetable oil such as coconut or olive oil in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture and seal tightly to create 500mg herbal capsules for post nasal drip relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with post nasal drip because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages. The menthol in peppermint oil stimulates the mucous membranes, thinning out excess mucus and promoting drainage. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory effects reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal tissues, allowing for easier breathing and relief from congestion. By addressing these underlying causes of post nasal drip, peppermint capsules provide fast and effective relief from discomfort and congestion.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol, a key constituent of peppermint oil, helps to thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion and postnasal drip.
Menthol: Menthol in peppermint has a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effect, which can help relieve sinus pressure and discomfort associated with postnasal drip.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint capsules have natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, including the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby reducing postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant are used due to their ability to relieve congestion and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, making them an effective ingredient in capsules for post nasal drip.
Buds: Peppermint buds are rich in menthol, which has natural decongestant properties that help to thin mucus and provide quick relief from postnasal drip symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried peppermint leaves mentha x piperita and empty gelatin capsules for use.
Measure one tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves and grind into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered peppermint with 500 milligrams of vegetable glycerin in a small bowl to create filling mixture.
Fill each capsule shell with approximately one-quarter teaspoon or five hundred milligrams of the peppermint-glycerin mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to six months at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain the extract of Eucalyptus globulus, a natural decongestant that effectively relieves congestion and excess mucus in the nasal passages. The capsules' active ingredients work to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel, thereby reducing the sensation of postnasal drip. Additionally, the eucalyptol present in the capsules has natural antibacterial properties that help combat underlying infections, further alleviating symptoms associated with postnasal drip.
remedy_properties: Mucolytic activity: Tasmanian Blue Gum's mucolytic properties help break down and thin excess mucus in the nasal passages, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion and postnasal drip.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in Tasmanian Blue Gum capsules have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can contribute to postnasal drip, helping to reduce inflammation and infection.

Expectorant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up phlegm and reducing the sensation of postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion and ease nasal passage irritation, making them effective in treating post nasal drip.
Barks: The barks contain compounds that have antimicrobial effects, which can help combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses that contribute to post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry eucalyptus globulus leaves in a well-ventilated area to preserve their medicinal properties for at least two weeks.
Grind dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered eucalyptus globulus with 200mg of gelatin capsules and a small amount of purified water to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, making sure each capsule contains an accurate dose of 250mg of eucalyptus globulus powder.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate them for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with post nasal drip because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause excessive mucus production, leading to post nasal drip. Additionally, thyme's decongestant and expectorant properties help to reduce nasal congestion and thin out mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving the constant sensation of a "runny nose" and coughing. This natural remedy provides relief from post nasal drip symptoms without harsh chemicals or artificial ingredients.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear excess mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and postnasal drip.

Antibacterial properties: Thyme's antibacterial properties can help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to postnasal drip, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy respiratory tract.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, providing relief from discomfort and congestion associated with postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of thyme contain a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate congestion and sinus pressure associated with post-nasal drip.
Buds: Thyme buds are rich in essential oils, particularly thymol, which has natural expectorant properties that help loosen mucus and reduce coughs related to post-nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers, measuring out 20 grams for this recipe.
Dry the harvested herbs in a warm oven at 150°f for four hours or until crispy.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 400 milligrams of thyme powder and store them in an airtight container.
Take two capsules twice daily, after meals, for eight weeks to alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Myrtle capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain the herb myrtle, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the nose and throat. By reducing inflammation, myrtle capsules can help to alleviate congestion and clear out excess mucus, providing relief from the uncomfortable sensation of postnasal drip. Expectorant properties: Myrtle's natural expectorants help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and nasal passages, relieving congestion and discomfort associated with postnasal drip.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Myrtle's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, sinuses, and throat, which can alleviate symptoms of postnasal drip such as a runny nose, sneezing, and coughing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality myrtus communis leaves in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the fresh leaves using a dehydrator or sun drying method for 24 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried myrtle leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 10:1 ratio of powdered myrtle with silica gel as filler and encapsulate using 500mg capsules.
Store the finished herbal capsules in an airtight container for up to 2 years at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain salvanic acid, a natural antihistamine that effectively reduces inflammation in the nasal passages. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in amounts sufficient for desired capsule strength.

Grind the dried herbal material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 250-500 milligrams of the powdered sage herb with filler materials such as rice flour or potato starch in 1000-milligram capsules.
Fill the prepared capsules with the mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with post nasal drip because of its natural antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and discomfort associated with postnasal drip. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers from your garden or purchase dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in bulk weighing 100 grams.
Dry the harvested flowers using a dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours to prevent loss of essential oils.
Grind the dried lavender flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered lavender with 200 milliliters of vegetable oil in a glass jar, sealing tightly and letting it steep for 2 weeks, shaking daily.

Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another glass jar, discarding the solids and filling capsules with 250 milligrams of the infused oil using a capsule machine or filling tool within 24 hours of straining.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena capsules helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the nasal passages. The herb's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, reducing congestion and promoting easier breathing. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and dry them in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for two hours.
Combine one part dried verbena officinalis leaves with one part dried peppermint leaves in a large bowl.
Grind the mixture into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for five minutes.
Mix one teaspoon of the powdered herbal blend with 250ml of boiling water to create an infusion that steeps for ten minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with one half teaspoon of the cooled infusion and store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony capsules helps with post nasal drip because they possess natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the nasal passages. The herb's ability to reduce inflammation and congestion alleviates the sensation of excessive mucus dripping down the back of the throat, providing relief from the discomfort and annoyance caused by postnasal drip. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The capsule's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, allowing mucus to drain more freely and alleviating postnasal drip symptoms.
Expectorant: The expectorant property of the capsules helps loosen and clear excess mucus from the throat and nasal passages, providing relief from coughing, congestion, and postnasal drip.

Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of the capsules help combat underlying bacterial infections that can contribute to postnasal drip, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting faster recovery.

remedy_recipe: Dry the dried peony root powder according to traditional chinese medicine standards within two hours of receiving it.
Combine one part dried peony root powder with three parts mulberry silk powder and mix well for at least 30 minutes.
Add five grams of the mixed powder mixture to a glass mortar and grind it into a fine powder using a pestle for about an hour.
Transfer the powdered mixture to a clean capsule machine and fill capsules to approximately two milliliters per filling within one minute.
Store the filled herbal chinese peony capsules in an airtight container at room temperature or below for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat and sinuses. remedy_recipe: Gather dried taraxacum officinale flowers and pods in a clean glass container for later use.
Measure out 20g of the dried herb and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground herb with 40g of silica gel capsules to create an even blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules by spooning the herbal blend into each capsule until they are approximately one-third full.
Label and store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass capsules helps with post nasal drip because of its natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and rinse them gently with cold water.
Dry the leaves in shade for 3-4 hours or until they become crumbly.
Combine 30g of dried leaves with 10g of rice flour in a small bowl.
Grind the mixture into fine powder using a coffee grinder for 1 minute.
Fill #00 gelatin capsules with 500mg of the powdered herbal blend and seal immediately.

This promotes a healthy balance of gut flora, which is linked to a stronger immune system and reduced inflammation in the nasal passages. By soothing and calming the mucous membranes, chicory capsules can help alleviate congestion, sinus pressure, and excess mucus production, providing relief from post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chicory's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and discomfort associated with postnasal drip.
Expectorant properties: The mucilage content of chicory capsules helps to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing the sensation of dripping or coughing up phlegm.

Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Chicory's bioactive compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and viruses that can contribute to postnasal drip, helping to prevent infections and reduce symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chicory roots contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the nasal passages.
Rhyzomes: Chicory rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and congestion associated with post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried cichorium intybus leaves from your local herb store or online supplier.
Grind the cichorium intybus leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the ground cichorium intybus with 50 milliliters of boiling distilled water to create an infusion.
Steep the mixture in a glass container at room temperature for 30 minutes before straining and cooling.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the cooled infusion and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain the essential oil of the herb, which has natural expectorant properties. When inhaled or ingested, rosemary oil stimulates the mucous membranes, helping to break down and clear out excess mucus and phlegm that can accumulate in the back of the throat, sinuses, and nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and discomfort associated with postnasal drip.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary's antimicrobial compounds help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can contribute to postnasal drip, reducing inflammation and congestion.

Decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects: Rosemary's volatile oils, such as camphor and borneol, have a natural decongestant effect, reducing swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, and alleviating postnasal drip symptoms like congestion and coughing.

Expectorant properties: The saponins and terpenoids in rosemary help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel, which can provide relief from postnasal drip and associated discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including post nasal drip, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Leaves: The leaves of rosemary contain camphor, a natural decongestant that helps relieve congestion and soothe the nasal passages associated with post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves from a trusted source or purchase them from a reputable supplier.
Grind 250mg of the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered rosmarinus officinalis with 500mg of empty capsule shells and fill capsules to desired size using a capsule filler machine or by hand.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 1 year to maintain potency.
Take 2-3 capsules twice daily with water as needed, starting with a low dose and gradually increasing as necessary to alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for post nasal drip are a natural supplement that helps alleviate the discomfort caused by excess mucus production in the nasal passages. These capsules typically contain ingredients such as slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root, which soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing congestion and sinus pressure. Examples of herbal capsules that help with post nasal drip include Nature's Bounty Sinus Relief and Gaia Herbs Postnasal Drip Formula. By taking these capsules, individuals can experience relief from constant coughing, sneezing, and congestion, allowing them to breathe easier and enjoy improved quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat post nasal drip with herbal capsules is to combine a capsule containing Echinacea, Golden Seal, and Slippery Elm. These herbs work together to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, thin mucus, and soothe the throat. Take 2-3 capsules three times a day, ideally during meals or before bed, to help alleviate congestion and promote a good night's sleep. Consistency is key for optimal relief from post nasal drip symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to post nasal drip that are treated with herbal capsules are sinusitis, bronchitis, and allergies. Herbal capsules containing ingredients such as turmeric, ginger, and boswellia can help alleviate congestion and inflammation in the respiratory tract. Capsules made from herbs like echinacea and elderberry may also boost immunity and reduce symptoms associated with upper respiratory infections. These natural remedies can provide relief without harsh side effects or dependency on prescription medications.
